title: The most disturbing moment in Evangelion
display_title: The most disturbing moment in Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: The most disturbing moment in Evangelion             Quote Post by Abaddon » Tue Jul 13, 2004 11:25 am I have allows been interested in learn what disturbs people, mentally of course. So I was wondering, what images in Eva disturb you? For me, the first time I saw lilith was shocking and very disturbing. And I also found the scene when the MP evas ate eva 02 to be disquieting.

[]:             Quote Post by secondson » Tue Jul 13, 2004 11:51 am Well, I was pretty psychologically damaged by the entirety of EoE...it that's what you mean...

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Tue Jul 13, 2004 11:57 am Well, due to my christian beliefs, y was pretty shocked when i saw Lilith for the first time. But it was only the first impression. :shock: It also was disturbing episode 23, when Rei died and we found out that she is one of many clones. :cry: But the most disturbing of all is... Shinji MASTURBATING, and that close up of his hand, that was a little too much. :?

[]:             Quote Post by EVA-01X » Tue Jul 13, 2004 11:57 am Shinji. Asuka. Hospital. Nuff' said.

[]:             Quote Post by quatre1453 » Tue Jul 13, 2004 12:24 pm when the eva series turend into Rei's and the one with half of its face and skull ripped off looks up at Shinji. that gave me the willies

[]:             Quote Post by RubberSoul » Tue Jul 13, 2004 12:29 pm I thought it was horribly disturbing to see Eva 02/Asuka torn apart by the Eva series.

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Langley » Tue Jul 13, 2004 12:39 pm Shinji+Hospital room+Comatose Asuka. That made me sick. And probably when Asuka was getting ripped apart near the end of her fight. Only because I noticed one of the Eva series licking her... or something to that degree of sickness.

[]: EOE             Quote Post by the-artist-known-as-chris » Tue Jul 13, 2004 12:40 pm EOE kicked the shit out of me emotionally the first time I saw it.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Tue Jul 13, 2004 1:09 pm When the giant Rei/Lilith's head broke off and blood sprayed across the sky. That scene freaked me out. I also found the scene where unit 01 breaks out of Leleil (the shadow angel) disturbing as well. Asuka's "my god, is that what I'm piloting" really hit a cord within me.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr.Guy » Tue Jul 13, 2004 2:55 pm I'd say the Reiquarium or Asuka's mind rape scene (directors cut). The former just grossed me out... but the latter was quite hard to watch (emotional).

[]:             Quote Post by max3000 » Tue Jul 13, 2004 3:04 pm There was a disturbing image flash from EoE that i could get outta my mind for a few days. It was a flash of Asuka's dead body with worms crawling in her face and skin... Umm... feel free to post a link to that pic

[]:             Quote Post by Esion » Tue Jul 13, 2004 3:35 pm Actually nothing in the series was extremly disturbing to me. However there was one scene in EoE that really got to me. There is some random Nerv worker on his knees and he's saying something like "oh...no...no...no" and then his brains are blown out. I have a fear of being executed like that. The masturbation scene and the rest of it weren't all that shocking.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Tue Jul 13, 2004 3:44 pm Asuka's mind rape scene, I hated Asuka until i saw that ep and in a twisted display of just about the worst thinkgs a child can ever hear a parent say i understood her. She kind of grew on me after that, when i realized all she wanted was some one to hold her and tell her she was special. EoE's hospital moment between Shinji and her is a close second though.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Tue Jul 13, 2004 3:50 pm The freeze-frame right after Misato's death in EoE disturbed me mostly because the animators actually bothered to add that in. If you don't know what I'm talking about freeze-frame it right after the area explodes, and see if you can find Misato's disconnected legs and torso.

[]:             Quote Post by max3000 » Tue Jul 13, 2004 3:52 pm oh oh i found the pic i was talking about! view at you'r own risk. http://www.ferge.net/pics/auska-dead.gif

[]:             Quote Post by Esion » Tue Jul 13, 2004 3:55 pm Phaze wrote: The freeze-frame right after Misato's death in EoE disturbed me mostly because the animators actually bothered to add that in. If you don't know what I'm talking about freeze-frame it right after the area explodes, and see if you can find Misato's disconnected legs and torso.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Tue Jul 13, 2004 4:33 pm The Asuka, Shinji hospital scene, more because I have a great disgust of hospitals.

[]:             Quote Post by coff » Tue Jul 13, 2004 5:00 pm There were a lot of scenes that had an emotional impact on me but I was never really disturbed by anything.

[]:             Quote Post by EVA-01X » Tue Jul 13, 2004 5:21 pm quatre1453 wrote: when the eva series turend into Rei's and the one with half of its face and skull ripped off looks up at Shinji. that gave me the willies

[]:             Quote Post by secondson » Tue Jul 13, 2004 6:04 pm How Eva has damaged me, let me count the ways... 7. Anytime Shinji chokes Asuka (or any Asuka-like person, like the apartment scene in EOE *shudders* That bothered me, and I don't know why) 6. Gendo's complementation in EOE 5. Anything involving Shinji's penis and a comatose Asuka is fairly upsetting to me (I have a deep-seated of fear sperm - I just can't help it) 4. The floating Reis, Kaworus, Yuis, etc., who were turning people into goo in EOE (you couldn't escape them - I'm sorry, that's just creepy) 3. Asuka's defeat at the hands of the mass production units (the sounds, man, the *sounds*! I felt so sick when I saw that...) 2. Kaji's death (I saw it coming a mile away, but I still jumped when I heard the bang - and the fact that he was smiling all the way to his death...it wasn't so much disturbing as it was hard for me to watch...yeah, I'm lame, whatever) 1. The entire concept of complementation - yeah, I get the symbolism and psychology behind it, but, y'know what? I'm old-school, and that bothers the shit out of me.
